Add commit-summary job to CI: show author, message, files changed

Adds a lightweight job that runs on every push and writes a summary
table to the GitHub Actions job summary page -- commit SHA, author,
message, and a git diff --stat of files changed. Makes CI notification
emails useful rather than generic workflow status pings.

Fix rolling deployment race: compare-and-delete Redis endpoint in cleanup

During rolling deployments, the old container's cleanup() was
unconditionally deleting the Redis endpoint key, even after the new
container had already registered its own IP. Now cleanup only deletes
the key if it still contains this container's own IP address.

feat(CS-030): pinned approval cards in Run Console + needs_input notice

fix: prevent duplicate card files from concurrent workers

_write() now scans for and deletes any stale files with the same card ID
before writing, preventing the case where multiple board workers load the
same card simultaneously and write it under different title slugs.

Also: check-dupes --fix now auto-deletes stale duplicates instead of
just reporting them.

in openclaw.json. When secrets were migrated to credentials/secrets.json
via SecretRef (OpenClaw 2026.3.2+), the regex found nothing → script
exited with error on every keepfresh run → all OAuth tokens expired and
9Router disabled the connections (kiro x3, iflow x2, ~35h of downtime).

The .open class sets transform:translateX(0) to slide it in.

so panels were technically visible but pushed 100% off-screen.

a reflow, and close functions use classList.remove('open') +
300ms timeout before hiding. Smooth transi

container, causing a full replacement cycle instead of a quick restart.

security: enterprise-grade hardening across engine

- Constant-time comparison: subtle::ConstantTimeEq for passphrase verification
- Poison recovery: unwrap_or_else on all lock operations
- Audit key: SIGNING_KEY wrapped in Zeroizing, generated with OsRng
- SSRF p

fix: resolve audit HMAC false tampering and dark mode sidebar readability

Audit log entries with multi-key JSONB detail objects were incorrectly
flagged as tampered. PostgreSQL JSONB reorders keys (by length, then
alphabetically), causing HMAC mismatch after DB roundtrip. Fix adds
canonical key sorting via sortKeys() before HMAC computation.

Also fixes active agent name being unreadable in dark mode sidebar
by adding appropriate dark mode background/text color variants.

fix: always full-sync reflections + insights to cloud (#638)

The incremental cursor meant that after a cloud restart (which wipes the
in-memory relay store), previously synced reflections/insights were lost
because the node only sent items newer than the cursor.

Now sends all items (max 200) every 60s. The cloud relay upserts by ID,
so duplicates are harmless. This keeps the cloud populated through
restarts.

fix: add connect timeout to pinned HTTP clients in web_fetch and http tools

Pinned clients (used for SSRF-safe DNS pinning) had no connect_timeout,
only a 30s overall timeout. When DNS resolves both IPv6 and IPv4 addresses
and IPv6 is unreachable, the entire timeout budget burns on the first
connection attempt with no fallback. Adding a 10s connect_timeout matches
the standard client pattern and allows fast failover to working addresses.

Include encryption_pubkey in invite format

Root cause: Ed25519 keys are NOT Curve25519 keys. When I encrypt
a message TO Jaka, I was using her Ed25519 key as if it were a
Curve25519 key. Jaka then tries to decrypt with her Curve25519
private key, but the keys don't match.

Fix: Include the Curve25519 encryption public key in the invite
so peers can encrypt TO each other correctly.

New invite format (7 parts):
  oc:v1;ed25519_pubkey;curve25519_pubkey;onion:port;name;ts;sig

fix: send deploy/restart notifications from host instead of agent container

The agent's result text was never delivered because pull_and_deploy uses
execSync (blocking the event loop) then process.exit(0), killing the
process before the container output could be sent. The restarting.flag
was also written from inside the container which was fragile.

Now the IPC handlers send notifications directly and write the restart
flag on the host side before any blocking work begins.

perf: replace urllib ThreadPoolExecutor with curl subprocess for API calls

The ThreadPoolExecutor wall-clock timeout approach failed: future.result(timeout)
returns, but the zombie urllib threads block Python's atexit handler. Even with
shutdown(wait=False) and os._exit(0), the timeout mechanism was unreliable.

subprocess.run(['curl', ...], timeout=N) uses SIGKILL after N seconds — a
guaranteed wall-clock timeout with no threads, no cleanup issues, no atexit hang.

- _github_api_get: curl -s -

joins all ThreadPoolExecutor threads at exit, blocking indefinitely when
threads are stuck on HTTP reads. os._exit(0) bypasses this.

fix(ci): flush profraw coverage data in E2E teardown (#550)

The ironclaw binary only handles SIGINT (via tokio::signal::ctrl_c),
not SIGTERM. When conftest.py sent SIGTERM during teardown, the OS
killed the process immediately without running atexit handlers, so
LLVM never flushed .profraw files. cargo llvm-cov report then found
zero profraw files and failed.

- Send SIGINT instead of SIGTERM so the existing ctrl_c handler
  triggers graceful shutdown → main() returns → atexit runs → profraw

fix: pass OpenRouter, Moonshot, email and IMAP secrets to container

The buildEnvVars() function was missing mappings for secrets that
are set in the Cloudflare Dashboard but were never forwarded to the
container process. This caused OPENROUTER_API_KEY, MOONSHOT_API_KEY,
EMAIL_*_PASSWORD, IMAP_HOST, IMAP_USER, and IMAP_PASSWORD to be
unavailable inside the container despite being configured.

fix: safe UTF-8 string truncation — prevent panic on multi-byte chars

using floor_char_boundary().

byte 200 inside a U+1F534 spanning bytes 198..202.

Fix macOS crash on quit: close SQLite before Dart VM teardown (v1.17.22)

Handle AppLifecycleState.detached to gracefully close the drift database
before the Dart VM shuts down. Without this, sqlite3's functionDestroy
runs on a DartWorker thread after Dart objects are freed, causing abort().

docs: 确认 OpenClaw 版本发布时间

Use separate Curve25519 keys for encryption (cross-platform fix)

Instead of deriving Curve25519 keys from Ed25519 (which varies by platform),
we now generate and store separate encryption keys.

Changes:
- Identity now generates/stores a Curve25519 keypair for encryption
- Handshakes include encryption_pubkey for the recipient to use
- Peer stores the encryption public key directly (no derivation)
- This ensures identical behavior on x86_64, ARM64, etc.

Fix cross-platform key derivation for ARM/x86 compatibility

Replace libsodium's to_curve25519_private_key() with deterministic
SHA256-based derivation. This ensures identical keys on all platforms
(x86_64, ARM64, etc.) since libsodium's conversion may vary by arch.

Both sides now use: SHA256(ed25519_key + 'curve25519') as Curve25519 seed

Remove atexit handler that caused daemon to die

The atexit.register(self.stop) in TorManager was causing the daemon
to shut down when the parent process exited. This made it impossible
to run the daemon in background without systemd.

Now the daemon stays running until explicitly stopped.

fix(wasm): coerce string parameters to schema-declared types (#498)

* fix(wasm): coerce string parameters to schema-declared types

LLMs frequently pass numeric values as JSON strings ("5" instead of 5)
or booleans as strings ("true" instead of true). The WASM module's
serde deserializer rejects these type mismatches. This adds a
coerce_params_to_schema() helper that walks the params JSON object
and converts string values to their schema-declared types (number,
integer, boolean) before passing

Change default permission mode from acceptEdits to bypassPermissions

Coding agents launched in headless mode with acceptEdits are extremely
limited — they cannot run tests, git operations, or installs. The default
should be bypassPermissions (full access) unless the user explicitly
requests sandboxed execution.

fix: canvas view reads current session + accepts live events + recent fallback

1. fetchCanvasComponents() to bail immediately (no session = no query)
2. canvas_push events to be silently dropped (session match failed)

- canvas/index.ts reads appState.currentSessionKey as fallback session
- canvas_push/canvas_update even

Fix Ed25519 to Curve25519 key conversion for encryption

- peer.py: Convert Ed25519 verify key to Curve25519 public key for Box
- agent.py: Use to_curve25519_private_key() instead of naive byte slice

The previous code tried to use Ed25519 keys directly with NaCl Box,
which expects Curve25519 keys. This caused decryption failures.

Now both sides properly convert:
- Sender: Ed25519 signing key -> Curve25519 private key (for Box)
- Recipient: Ed25519 verify key -> Curve25519 public key (for Box)

fix: ThreadPoolExecutor context manager blocks on shutdown, defeating wall-clock timeout

The 'with ThreadPoolExecutor() as pool:' pattern calls pool.shutdown(wait=True)
on exit, which blocks until the background thread finishes — even after
future.result(timeout=N) raised TimeoutError. This meant slow/stuck
urllib calls were never actually abandoned.

Fix: use pool.shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True) in a finally block
so the caller returns immediately on timeout.

Fix AEAD decryption: use full RTP header as AAD for rtpsize mode

The aead_xchacha20_poly1305_rtpsize mode requires the full variable-length
RTP header (including CSRC entries and extensions) as AAD. We were only
using the first 12 bytes (base header), but incoming packets from other
Discord clients include RTP header extensions (X bit set), making the
full header 20+ bytes. This AAD mismatch caused every decrypt to fail.

Root cause from debug logs:
- Incoming packets have byte[0]=0x90 (X=1, ex

fix: Use prebuild-install for faster better-sqlite3 setup

Improved strategy for obtaining better-sqlite3 native binary:
1. Check if binary already exists (cached)
2. Try prebuild-install to download precompiled binary (~2s)
3. Fall back to npm rebuild to compile from source (~30s)

This significantly reduces first-install time when precompiled
binaries are available for the platform.

fix: Auto-rebuild better-sqlite3 on plugin load

Problem:
- OpenClaw uses --ignore-scripts during plugin installation for security
- This prevents better-sqlite3's install script and postinstall from running
- Users get 'Could not locate the bindings file' error

Solution:
- Check if better-sqlite3 native addon is available on plugin load
- If not found, automatically rebuild it using npm rebuild
- This happens once on first load, transparent to user

Technical details:
- Use try/require to dete

fix: replace NWListener with FlyingFox to eliminate HTTP 400 body parsing bug

The NWListener-based server did a single connection.receive() assuming
the full HTTP body arrived in one TCP packet. URLSession's connection
reuse / chunked encoding split bodies across packets, causing truncated
JSON → intermittent "Invalid command" 400 errors.

FlyingFox handles HTTP framing (Content-Length) properly, fixing the
root cause. Also adds CLI Makefile (make install), renames bin/atl to
bin/atl-sim to avo

fix(feishu): convert audio type to file for API compatibility

Feishu's GetMessageResource API only accepts 'image' or 'file' as the
type parameter. When downloading voice messages, nanobot was passing
'audio' which caused the API to reject the request with an error.

This fix converts 'audio' to 'file' in _download_file_sync method
before making the API call, allowing voice messages to be downloaded
and transcribed successfully.

Fixes voice message download failure in Feishu channel.

Fix macOS CI: allow memory pct tolerance, remove dead variable

- Memory usage cross-validation now allows ±2% tolerance since available
  memory can change between separate function calls (race on macOS CI)
- Remove unused likely_cause variable flagged by ShellCheck

thinking model type via a new `isGemini31FlashLiteModel` detector.

and the GeoLite2-City.mmdb into the camoufox package directory
(/usr/local/lib/.../camoufox/). The package dir is owned by root,
causing PermissionError when running as the browser user.

chown the camoufox package dir to browser before the fetch.

fix: replace curl-subprocess LLM call in feishu.py with call_llm from websocket

The curl-based approach had multiple issues:
- Empty stdout on large payloads causing JSONDecodeError
- Different streaming/non-streaming behavior vs websocket path
- Fragile argument passing prone to type errors

Now reuses call_llm() from websocket.py which uses httpx and is battle-tested
across all providers (OpenRouter/Claude, Qwen, DeepSeek etc).

Fix nginx crash loop: use Docker DNS resolver for deferred upstream resolution

Nginx was failing at startup with "host not found in upstream" because
it resolves upstream hostnames at config-load time. Switched to
variable-based proxy_pass with resolver 127.0.0.11 (Docker embedded DNS)
so resolution happens at request time, not startup.

publishing moltguard, which caused the published package to be missing

fix(tui): Detach stdin handler before Ink subapp dynamic imports

When opening the settings Ink subapp via 'a' or 'p', the screen was set
to "ink-subapp" synchronously but the onData handler stayed registered
during async dynamic imports. dispatch() silently dropped all keystrokes
for the "ink-subapp" screen, causing input to never reach the Ink app.

Move process.stdin.removeListener("data", onData) to run before the
dynamic imports so buffered stdin data flows to the Ink proxy stream.

fix(ci): prevent edited events from creating skipped CI runs (#13170)

### What this PR does

Before this PR:
Editing a PR's title or body triggers the `edited` event in the CI
workflow. Although job-level `if` conditions skip execution, GitHub
still creates a new workflow run with "skipped" status. Since GitHub UI
shows the latest run, this hides the real CI results from the previous
`opened`/`synchronize` run.

After this PR:
- Removed `edited` from CI workflow trigger types, so title/body edi

fix: MiniMax-M2.5 将工具调用嵌入 thinking 块导致大任务中断

问题：MiniMax-M2.5 有时将 <minimax:tool_call> 嵌入 thinking 块内而非
作为独立的 tool_use 块返回，导致系统误判为"无工具调用+无文本"，
触发 no_confirmation_text 中断机制，用户看到"工具已执行但多次未返回
任何可见文本确认"错误。

修复（三层防御）：

1. Provider 层（根治）：
   - anthropic.py: 检查 thinking 块内嵌入的文本格式工具调用
   - openai.py: 检查 reasoning_content 内嵌入的工具调用

2. Engine/Agent 层（防御兜底）：
   - reasoning_engine._parse_decision(): 二次检查 thinking 内容
   - agent.py IM 路径: 同步添加 thinking 工具调用检查

3. 容错恢复：
   - max_confirmation_text_retries 从 1 提升到

fix(telegram): add missing webhook section to capabilities.json (#381)

The Telegram channel capabilities file was missing the `webhook`
block inside `capabilities.channel`, causing the router to fall back
to the default `X-Webhook-Secret` header instead of the Telegram-
specific `X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token`.

When a webhook secret is configured (via `telegram_webhook_secret`),
incoming updates are rejected with 401 because Telegram sends the
token in `X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token` but t

fix: atomic claim prevents scheduled tasks from executing twice (#657)

* fix: atomic claim prevents scheduled tasks from executing twice (#138)

Replace the two-phase getDueTasks() + deferred updateTaskAfterRun() with
an atomic SQLite transaction (claimDueTasks) that advances next_run
BEFORE dispatching tasks to the queue. This eliminates the race window
where subsequent scheduler polls re-discover in-progress tasks.

Key changes:
- claimDueTasks(): SELECT + UPDATE in a single db.transaction(),

feat: v4.1.1 — Smart input detection in patrol

When patrol captures tmux output, it now detects 4 types of worker blocks:

1. permission (y/n/approve) → auto-respond 'y'
2. proceed (press to continue) → auto-respond 'y'
3. selection (choose/pick/option) → write to state file for LLM
4. error (command not found, failed) → write to state file for LLM

Simple cases (permission/proceed) are auto-handled.
Complex cases (selection/error) are flagged in state file with:
  - needs_input: true
  - input

fix(utils): Align getUptime fallback with isReachablePing

The getUptime() fallback path only counted code "200" pings, while
the metrics-cache path counted both "200" and "401" via isOkPing.
This caused inconsistent uptime percentages when the cache was
disabled. Use isReachablePing consistently in both paths.

Also remove the deprecated isOkPing alias and replace all three
internal call sites with isReachablePing directly.
